lessons.title: Family Tree
lessons.topic: Looking at a family photo album
lessons.context: Looking at a family photo album.

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Who do you see in family photos?
questions.hint: Think about the people who live in your house with you!

questions.question_text: Where do we keep our family photos?
questions.hint: It's a special book where we put pictures to remember happy times!

questions.question_text: What are people doing in family photos?
questions.hint: Family photos show happy memories! What do happy faces look like?

items.definition: A girl child of a mother or father.

steps.title: Emma's Family Album
steps.text: Emma looks at her family photo album with Mum. She sees her baby brother Tom. Emma is her parent's daughter. Tom is their son. Emma says, 'Look! There are two children in our family!'
steps.scene_description: young girl and mother looking at photo album together on sofa, smiling, cozy living room

questions.question: Who looks at the photo album with Emma?
Is Tom Emma's baby brother?
How many children are in Emma's family?

steps.explanation: We use 's after a name to show that something belongs to that person.

items.explanation: We need 's to show it belongs to Ben

items.sentence: I see two baby in the photo.
items.explanation: Use 'babies' for more than one baby
Correct! We use 's to show belonging
We need 's to show it belongs to Tom

items.question: This is ___ baby brother.
items.explanation: Use 's after the name to show belonging

items.question: I see three ___ in the photo.
items.explanation: Children is the plural of child

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today we're looking at Lucy's family photo album. Look at this picture! Lucy's mum and dad are her parents. Lucy has a baby brother called Max. He is their son. Lucy is their daughter. Who can you see in your family photos?

questions.question: Whose photo album are they looking at?
What is the baby brother's name?
Who are Lucy's parents?

steps.prompt: Look at your family photo and tell me about it! Practice saying these sentences:
steps.expected_text: This is my mum's photo album

prompts.expected_text: This is my mum's photo album

prompts.prompt: Say: I can see [number] people in the photo.
prompts.expected_text: I can see three people in the photo
They are my parents and my baby brother

questions.question_text: A boy in the family is called a ___.
This is ___ photo album. (Use Emma)
I have two ___. (Use baby)
True or False: Mum and Dad are called parents.
Which is correct?

lessons.title: Relatives
lessons.topic: Talking about visitors coming for dinner
lessons.context: Talking about visitors coming for dinner.

steps.instruction: Let's think about when family comes to visit! Answer these questions.

questions.question_text: Who comes to visit your house for dinner?
questions.hint: Think about your family members who are older and love you very much!

questions.question_text: What do we do before visitors come for dinner?
questions.hint: We want the house to look nice and tidy for our guests!

questions.question_text: When grandma arrives at the door, what do you say?
questions.hint: We use friendly, polite words to greet people we love!

items.definition: A child of your aunt or uncle.

items.hint: Your dad's brother or your mom's brother
Your mom's sister or your dad's sister
Your aunt or uncle's son or daughter

items.sentence: Uncle Tom is coming to visit. My ___ is very kind.
I play with my ___ at family dinners.
My ___ brings me presents on my birthday.

steps.title: Dinner with Family
steps.text: Today my aunt and uncle are coming for dinner. My cousin Emma is coming too. We are cleaning the house. Mom is cooking chicken and rice. Emma and I will play together after dinner. I am so excited!
steps.scene_description: happy family preparing dining table together for dinner, parents and children setting plates and food

questions.question: Who is coming for dinner?
What is Mom cooking?
What will the child and Emma do after dinner?

steps.rule_pattern: This is ___'s ___. / Who are they? They are my ___s.
steps.explanation: We use 's to show that something belongs to someone, and we add 's' to make more than one person in a family.
examples: This is Emma's cousin. He is coming for dinner.
Who are they? They are my aunts and uncles.
Look at Ben's family. His cousins are here!

items.sentence: Who are they? They are my ___.
My two ___ are coming for dinner.
___ are they? They are my family.

items.explanation: We need 's to show it belongs to Tom
Correct! We use 's to show possession

items.sentence: Who are they? They is my aunts.
items.corrected: Who are they? They are my aunts.

items.sentence: Sara's uncles are coming for dinner.
items.explanation: Perfect! Sara's shows possession and uncles is plural

items.question: This is ___ father.
items.explanation: Use 's after the name to show belonging

items.question: My two ___ are here.
items.explanation: Add 's' to make plural (more than one)

items.question: Look at ___ uncle!
items.explanation: Use 's to show it's Ben's uncle

steps.audio_text: Hello children! Today I want to tell you about my family dinner. My aunt Lisa and my uncle Tom are coming to my house tonight. They are bringing their daughter. She is my cousin Sarah. We are so excited! My mom is cooking spaghetti for dinner. Who are they? They are my favorite relatives!

questions.question: Who is coming for dinner?
What is the cousin's name?
What is mom cooking?

steps.prompt: Practice talking about family visitors! Listen and repeat each sentence.
steps.expected_text: My aunt is coming for dinner

prompts.prompt: Say: My aunt is coming for dinner.
prompts.expected_text: My aunt is coming for dinner

prompts.prompt: Say: Who are they? They are my cousins.
prompts.expected_text: Who are they? They are my cousins

questions.question_text: Your mom's sister is your ___.
This is ___ father. He is coming for dinner.
Who are they? They are my ___.
Read: 'My uncle and aunt are coming for dinner tonight. Their son is my cousin Tom.' Who is Tom?
True or False: 'My aunts is here' is correct.

steps.instruction: Let's think about people at the park! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Who can you see at a park?
questions.hint: Think about who goes to the park to have fun!

questions.question_text: What do people wear to the park?
questions.hint: People wear clothes that are good for running and playing!

questions.question_text: What can you say about a person at the park?
questions.hint: We describe people, not things! Think about how people look.

steps.title: At the Park
steps.text: Emma is at the park. She sees a man and a woman on the bench. The woman is Ben's mother. Three children are playing. One person has a red ball.
steps.scene_description: diverse families with children playing at sunny park, parents sitting on bench watching kids

questions.question: Where is Emma?
Who is the woman on the bench?
How many children are playing?

steps.rule_pattern: This is _____'s _____. (Emma's ball) / Who are they? They are _____. / one child → two children
steps.explanation: We use 's to show that something belongs to someone, and we change words when there is more than one person or thing.
examples: This is Tom's mother. She is at the park.
Who are they? They are children.
I see one woman and two men.

items.explanation: Use 's to show something belongs to Sara
The plural of child is children
The plural of man is men
Correct! We use 's to show possession
The plural of woman is women
Correct! Emma's shows the ball belongs to Emma
The plural of person is people

items.question: This is ___ mother.
items.explanation: Use 's after the name to show possession

items.question: I see two ___.
items.explanation: Women is the plural of woman

items.question: The ___ are playing.
items.explanation: Children is the plural of child

items.question: That is my ___.
items.explanation: Use father's to show the car belongs to father

steps.audio_text: Look at this picture of the park! Can you see the man with the blue shirt? That's Ben's father. Next to him is a woman with long hair. She's Ben's mother. How many children are in the park? I can see three children playing with a ball. One person is sitting under the tree.

questions.question: Who is the man with the blue shirt?
How many children are playing in the park?
Where is one person sitting?

steps.prompt: Look at the picture and describe the people at the park. Use the words you learned!

prompts.prompt: Point to someone and say: This is ___'s mother.
Look at the people playing and say: I can see ___ children.
Point to some people and ask: Who are they?
prompts.expected_text: Who are they? They are children.

questions.question_text: Which word means more than one child?
This is Tom___ father.
Read: 'I see a man and a woman at the park.' How many people do you see?
Who ___ they?
True or False: The word 'person' means one man or one woman.

lessons.title: Unit Review
lessons.topic: Identifying family members and using possessive 's
lessons.context: Identifying family members and using possessive 's.

steps.instruction: Let's think about families! Answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Who is your mom's mom?
questions.hint: She's older and gives you hugs and cookies!

questions.question_text: Look at the picture. This is Ben. The woman is Ben's mother. We can say: This is _____ mother.
questions.hint: We add 's to show something belongs to someone!

questions.question_text: Emma has a brother. His name is Tom. How do we say whose brother Tom is?
questions.hint: Remember to use 's after the person's name!

items.definition: A boy child in a family
A girl child in a family
A boy child in a family
A girl child in a family

items.question: What do we call a boy child in a family?
What do we call a girl child in a family?
What do we call your aunt or uncle's child?
What do we call a very young child?
What do we call a mother or father?
What word means more than one child?

steps.title: Lucy's Family Photo
steps.text: This is Lucy's family photo. Lucy's mother and father are smiling. Lucy's baby brother is sitting on the floor. Lucy's aunt and uncle are standing behind them. Who are they? They are Lucy's cousins! There are five children in the photo.
steps.scene_description: happy family portrait with parents, children, aunt, uncle and cousins posing together indoors

questions.question: Where is Lucy's baby brother?
How many children are in the photo?
Who is standing behind Lucy's parents?

steps.explanation: We use 's after a person's name to show that something belongs to them.

items.sentence: My ___ brother is two years old.

items.explanation: Use plural 's' with numbers greater than one
Use 's to show the cat belongs to Sara
Correct! We use 's after the name
Correct! We use 's to show belonging

items.question: This is ___ aunt.
items.explanation: Use 's after the name to show belonging

items.question: Look at ___!
items.explanation: Use 's to show the baby belongs to Sara

steps.audio_text: Hello everyone! Look at this family photo. This is my friend Tom's family. Tom's mother is wearing a blue dress. Tom's father is very tall. Who are they? They are Tom's cousins! Tom has three cousins. The baby is Tom's little sister. She is one year old.

questions.question: How many cousins does Tom have?
Who is wearing a blue dress?
How old is Tom's baby sister?

steps.prompt: Let's practice talking about families! Listen and repeat each sentence.

questions.question_text: Who is your parent's brother?
This is Tom___ mother.
I have three ___. (more than one cousin)
___ are they? They are my aunt and uncle.
True or False: 'Emma's father' means the father belongs to Emma.
